英検準1級 Speaking Practice 02
Grade Pre-1 準1級
試験の説明 | Instructions
⏱
Preparation
1 minute
🎙
Narration
2 minutes
📖 Story Topic
This is a story about a man who transformed an abandoned plot of land in his city into a community garden.
✏️ Your story should begin with this sentence
One day, a man was walking through his neighborhood when he noticed an empty, overgrown lot.
イラスト | Story Panels
 
1
Three weeks later
2
Several months later
3
A few weeks later
4
タイマー | Timers
⏱ Preparation Time
1:00
🎙 Narration Time
2:00
模範ナレーション | Model Narration
One day, a man was walking through his neighborhood when he noticed an empty, overgrown lot. The land was full of weeds and rubbish, and an old fence with a "No Entry" sign surrounded it. The man thought it was a waste of good space. He talked to his neighbours and got permission from the city. Then a group of volunteers came to help clean it up. They pulled out weeds, dug the soil, and built garden beds. The man worked hard with a spade while the others helped around him. Several months later, the empty lot had become a beautiful community garden. Tomatoes, carrots, and sunflowers were growing in the beds. A sign on the fence said "City Garden." Families came to look after the plants and pick the vegetables together. The man was very happy. A few weeks later, however, he found a notice on the fence from the City Council. It said the land would be turned into a car park in four weeks. He stood in front of the sign with his mouth open, unable to believe what he was reading.
質問と模範解答 | Questions & Model Answers
No. 1
Please look at the fourth picture. If you were the man, what would you be thinking?
Model Answer
I'd be thinking, "I cannot let the garden be destroyed. Everyone worked so hard to build it. I will collect signatures from people in the area and ask the city council to change their decision. I think if enough people speak up, the council will listen."
Now, Mr./Ms. ——, please turn over the card and put it down.
No. 2
Should urban planners prioritize green spaces over infrastructure such as parking lots and roads?
Model Answer
Yes, I think they should. Parks and gardens are good for people's health and make cities more pleasant. Also, many people now use trains or bicycles, so large car parks are not as important as before. It is better to use empty land to make green spaces that the whole community can enjoy.
No. 3
Do you think community-led projects can effectively address social problems that governments fail to solve?
Model Answer
Yes, I think they often can. When people in a community work together, they can fix local problems quite quickly. However, for large problems like housing or poverty, community groups alone cannot do enough. In those cases, it is important for the government to help as well.
No. 4
Some people argue that individuals do not have enough power to influence decisions made by local governments. Do you agree?
Model Answer
Not completely. One person alone may find it hard to change things, but when many people work together, it is possible. For example, if people sign a petition or go to a local meeting, the government often listens. I think it is important to keep trying and not give up.
